extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won the sprint after Soudal Quick-Step unexpectedly led with BALLERINI instead of Jakobsen, beating Groenewegen on the line
tactical_patterns: Soudal Quick-Step switched from Jakobsen to Ballerini as designated sprinter, suggesting internal team hierarchy shift during stage race.
DSM's Dainese rode leadout for Meerhoff but jumped for himself, revealing stage 7 freedom for domestiques when GC secured.
Bauhaus positioned wide at chicane 750m out, demonstrating positioning at technical features matters more than pure speed on circuit finishes.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: doubled stage wins, capitalized on Quick-Step internal confusion and Bauhaus's wide positioning error
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: neutral: strong positioning but narrowly beaten, showing Jayco sprint train still competitive without Hayter
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: negative: Quick-Step rode for Ballerini instead, clear signal of lost confidence or form during race week
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: positive: podium finish when jumping from leadout role suggests future protected sprinter status at DSM
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: negative: went too wide at 750m chicane despite defending this stage last year, tactical regression
